claims.text: The global IT Service Management (ITSM) software market size is projected to grow from USD 4.53 Billion in 2026 to USD 9.58 Billion by 2035 at a CAGR of 8.7%.
claims.evidence: The global it service management (itsm) software market size, valued at USD 4.53 Billion in 2026, is expected to climb to USD 9.58 Billion by 2035 at a CAGR of 8.7% during the forecast period from 2026 to 2035.

claims.text: A significant portion of ITSM implementations, over 72%, is aimed at enhancing service delivery, indicating a structural pain in service management processes.
claims.evidence: More than 72% of enterprises adopt ITSM solutions to enhance service delivery.

claims.text: The global IT Service Management (ITSM) software market is expected to grow significantly, reaching USD 76983.68 million by 2035, indicating strong demand for ITSM solutions.
claims.evidence: The IT Service Management (ITSM) Software Market is projected to grow from USD 15917.7 million in 2026 to USD 18964.35 million in 2027, reaching USD 76983.68 million by 2035, expanding at a CAGR of 19.14% during the forecast period.

claims.text: The IT service management (ITSM) software market shows a willingness to pay, as organizations are increasingly adopting cloud-based solutions with 64% migrating from on-premise systems to SaaS ITSM tools.
claims.evidence: Adoption of cloud-based ITSM platforms has grown significantly, with 64% of businesses migrating from on-premise systems to SaaS ITSM tools.

claims.text: Evidence indicates a degree of maturity in the ITSM category, with 78% of enterprises using at least one ITSM platform, highlighting strong market penetration.
claims.evidence: Surveys show that 78% of enterprises use at least one ITSM platform, and 46% of companies employ three or more ITSM modules for service delivery, highlighting strong multi-tool adoption.

claims.text: Automated ticketing systems now account for 62% of service operations, indicating an incumbent posture towards using advanced tools to improve efficiency.
claims.evidence: IT Service Management (ITSM) Software Market Analysis indicates that automated ticketing systems now account for 62% of service operations, reducing manual effort by 30–40% in large enterprises with more than 5,000 employees.
